title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) - Authentic Indian Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Master the classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow recipe. Get creamy, rich, and authentic flavors at home. Perfect for dinner!
longTailKeywords: best Butter Chicken recipe at home
Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani recipe
h2Headings: How to Make Butter Chicken Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of authentic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) garnished with fresh cilantro and cream.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich and creamy flavors of homemade Butter Chicken, a true Indian classic.

description: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian delicacy known for its rich, creamy tomato-based sauce and tender pieces of marinated chicken. This iconic dish offers a perfect balance of tanginess, sweetness, and mild spice, creating an unforgettable culinary experience. It is a comforting and luxurious meal, ideal for special occasions or a delightful weeknight dinner.

ingredients.notes: boneless, skinless thighs or breast, cut into 1-inch pieces
from fresh ripe tomatoes or canned
(made from 1/4 cup cashews soaked in hot water for 15 mins and blended)

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric powder, red chili powder, garam masala, salt, and lemon juice. Mix well to coat all the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Marinated Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken pieces in a single layer, ensuring not to overcrowd the pan. Cook until lightly browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Makhani Gravy Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, add 2 tablespoons of butter. Once melted, add the finely chopped onion and sauté until translucent and lightly golden, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining ginger-garlic paste and green chilies (if using), and cook for 1 minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Tomato Gravy
instructions.text: Add the tomato puree to the pan. Stir in the remaining red chili powder, garam masala, and salt. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook for 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the tomato mixture thickens and the oil starts to separate.

instructions.title: Blend and Refine the Gravy
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the cooked tomato mixture to a blender. Add the cashew paste and blend until completely smooth. You may need to add a splash of water if it is too thick. Strain the gravy back into the pan through a fine-mesh sieve for an extra smooth texture, pressing all the solids through.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the smooth gravy to the pan. Add the cooked chicken, remaining 2 tablespoons of butter, sugar, and crushed kasoori methi. Simmer gently for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the chicken to absorb the gravy.

instructions.title: Serve
instructions.text: Stir in the heavy cream just before serving. Do not boil after adding cream. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with naan, roti, or basmati rice.

tips: For extra tender chicken, marinate overnight. You can also lightly grill or pan-fry the chicken for a smoky flavor before adding to the gravy.

Soaking cashews in hot water for 15-20 minutes before blending helps achieve a super smooth and creamy texture for the gravy. For a nut-free alternative, you can use melon seeds paste or skip it, though the richness will be slightly different.
Adjust the sugar and red chili powder to your preference. Authentic Butter Chicken has a subtle sweetness and mild spice. A pinch of cardamom powder can also be added for extra aroma.
article:
Butter Chicken, known in India as Murgh Makhani, is a culinary masterpiece that has captivated palates worldwide. This iconic Indian dish, with its rich, creamy tomato-based gravy and tender pieces of marinated chicken, embodies comfort and luxury. It's more than just a meal; it's an experience, a symphony of flavors that balances sweet, tangy, and subtly spicy notes. Whether you’re a seasoned cook or a curious foodie, delving into the world of authentic Murgh Makhani is a rewarding journey that promises to elevate your home cooking to new heights.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, literally meaning "chicken with butter," is a celebrated dish originating from Delhi, India, in the 1950s. It was reputedly invented by Kundan Lal Gujral, the founder of Moti Mahal restaurant, who sought a way to utilize leftover tandoori chicken. The solution was brilliant: simmer the smoky chicken pieces in a luscious, velvety gravy made from tomatoes, butter, cream, and a medley of aromatic spices.

The hallmark of authentic Butter Chicken is its distinct flavor profile and texture. The sauce is typically smooth, often strained to remove any fibrous bits from the tomatoes or onions, resulting in a silken consistency. It boasts a beautiful reddish-orange hue, derived from tomatoes and sometimes a touch of Kashmiri chili powder, which imparts color without excessive heat. The chicken itself is traditionally marinated in yogurt and spices before being cooked in a tandoor (clay oven), giving it a characteristic smoky char that adds depth to the final dish. This combination of smoky chicken and a rich, creamy sauce is what makes Murgh Makhani an unparalleled classic among Indian chicken recipes.

## Ingredients for Authentic Butter Chicken

Crafting a truly authentic Butter Chicken requires attention to quality ingredients and a precise balance of spices. Here’s a breakdown of the essentials:

*   **For the Chicken Marinade:**
    *   **Chicken:** Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into bite-sized pieces. Thighs are often preferred for their tenderness.
    *   **Yogurt:** Plain, full-fat yogurt helps tenderize the chicken and holds the spices.
    *   **Ginger-Garlic Paste:** Freshly made for the best flavor.
    *   **Spices:** Red chili powder (Kashmiri for color, regular for heat), turmeric powder, garam masala, cumin powder, and salt.
*   **For the Makhani (Butter) Sauce:**
    *   **Tomatoes:** Fresh, ripe, and juicy tomatoes are crucial. Canned crushed tomatoes or tomato purée can also be used for convenience.
    *   **Onions:** Finely chopped or puréed, they form a base for the sauce.
    *   **Aromatics:** Fresh ginger and garlic, often in paste form.
    *   **Cashews or Almonds:** Soaked cashews or blanched almonds are blended into the sauce to provide richness and thickness.
    *   **Butter (Lots of it!):** Unsalted butter is essential for the dish's signature richness.
    *   **Cream:** Heavy cream or full-fat dairy cream adds to the luxurious texture.
    *   **Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves):** A non-negotiable ingredient, providing a unique, slightly bitter, aromatic depth.
    *   **Spices:** Garam masala, red chili powder, turmeric, cumin, coriander powder. Whole spices like cardamom pods, cloves, and cinnamon sticks are often used in the initial tempering.
    *   **Sweetener:** A touch of sugar or honey balances the acidity of the tomatoes.
    *   **Garnish:** Fresh coriander leaves and a drizzle of cream.

## How to Make Butter Chicken Step-by-Step

Making Butter Chicken might seem complex, but breaking it down into steps makes it manageable and incredibly rewarding.

1.  **Marinate the Chicken:** Combine chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric, garam masala, cumin, and salt. Mix well and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 4-6 hours, or even overnight for maximum flavor and tenderness.
2.  **Cook the Chicken:** Traditionally, marinated chicken is cooked in a tandoor. At home, you can grill it, pan-fry it until lightly charred, or bake it in a hot oven (around 200°C/400°F) until cooked through and slightly browned. Set aside.
3.  **Prepare the Makhani Sauce Base:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, melt some butter. Add whole spices (if using), then sauté chopped onions until translucent. Stir in ginger-garlic paste and cook until fragrant. Add chopped tomatoes, soaked cashews (or almonds), red chili powder, turmeric, cumin, and coriander powder. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the tomatoes soften and release their juices, and the mixture thickens – about 15-20 minutes.
4.  **Blend and Strain the Sauce:** Let the cooked tomato mixture cool slightly. Transfer it to a blender and blend until absolutely smooth. For an ultra-silky texture, pass the blended sauce through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing down to extract all the liquid and discarding any remaining solids. This is a key step for authentic Butter Chicken.
5.  **Simmer the Sauce:** Return the strained sauce to the pot. Bring it to a gentle simmer. Stir in garam masala, a pinch of sugar or honey, and a generous amount of butter. Continue to simmer for 10-15 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken further.
6.  **Combine Chicken and Sauce:** Add the cooked chicken pieces to the simmering sauce. Stir well to coat the chicken.
7.  **Finish and Garnish:** Stir in the heavy cream and crushed kasuri methi (rubbed between your palms to release its aroma). Simmer for another 5 minutes to heat through, ensuring the cream doesn't curdle. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish with fresh coriander leaves and an extra swirl of cream before serving.

## Tips for the Best Butter Chicken

Achieving that restaurant-quality Butter Chicken at home is entirely possible with a few expert tips:

*   **Marination is Key:** Don't skimp on marination time. The longer the chicken marinates, the more tender and flavorful it will be.
*   **Char the Chicken:** If you don't have a tandoor, pan-frying or grilling the chicken until it has a slight char will mimic the smoky flavor that defines classic Murgh Makhani.
*   **Blend and Strain:** This step is crucial for the velvety smooth texture that Butter Chicken is famous for. Don't skip it if you want a truly authentic experience.
*   **Quality Tomatoes:** Use the best quality ripe tomatoes you can find, as they form the backbone of the sauce.
*   **Don't Fear the Butter and Cream:** While you can lighten it, the "butter" in Butter Chicken is there for a reason! It contributes significantly to the richness and flavor.
*   **Kasuri Methi:** This dried fenugreek leaf adds a unique, irreplaceable aroma. Always crush it between your palms before adding to release its essential oils.
*   **Balance Flavors:** Taste your sauce frequently. Adjust sweetness (sugar/honey), tang (more tomato or a tiny bit of lemon juice), and salt to achieve the perfect balance.
*   **Let it Rest:** Like many stews and curries, Butter Chicken often tastes even better the next day, as the flavors have more time to deepen and meld.

## Serving Suggestions for Murgh Makhani

Butter Chicken is a versatile dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of accompaniments, making it a feast for any occasion.

*   **Breads:** The most classic pairing is with warm, fluffy naan bread, perfect for scooping up every last drop of the rich sauce. Tandoori roti, chapati, or paratha are also excellent choices.
*   **Rice:** Steamed basmati rice is a staple, allowing the flavors of the Murgh Makhani to shine. For an aromatic touch, serve with jeera rice (cumin rice) or saffron rice.
*   **Accompaniments:** A simple side salad, cooling raita (yogurt dip), or pickled onions can provide a refreshing contrast to the richness of the curry. A wedge of lemon can be offered to brighten the flavors further.
*   **Drinks:** A mango lassi or a light, crisp beer complements the meal beautifully.

## Butter Chicken Variations & Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is beloved, there are several ways to adapt it to dietary needs or personal preferences.

*   **Vegetarian Alternatives:** For those who don't eat meat, **Paneer Makhani** is a fantastic option, replacing chicken with cubes of Indian cottage cheese. Tofu or mixed vegetables can also be used to create a delightful vegetarian Murgh Makhani style curry.
*   **Healthier Versions:** To reduce fat, you can use less butter and cream, opting for low-fat dairy cream or a touch of Greek yogurt at the end. Grilling or baking the chicken instead of pan-frying also cuts down on oil. You can also increase the vegetable content in the sauce base.
*   **Spice Level:** Adjust the amount of red chili powder to your preference. For more heat, add green chilies or a pinch of cayenne. For less, stick to Kashmiri chili powder for color without much spice.
*   **Nut-Free Options:** If you have a nut allergy, you can substitute the cashews or almonds with sunflower seeds, melon seeds, or even a tablespoon of cornstarch slurry to thicken the sauce. Coconut milk can also add richness without nuts, though it will slightly alter the traditional flavor profile.
*   **Different Meats:** While less common for Murgh Makhani, some variations might use lamb or fish, though the cooking times and preparation methods would need to be adjusted accordingly.

Butter Chicken remains a timeless classic for a reason. Its harmonious blend of flavors and luxurious texture makes it a perennial favorite. By following these guidelines, you can recreate the magic of authentic Murgh Makhani in your own kitchen, impressing family and friends with this truly iconic Indian chicken dish. Enjoy the journey of making and savoring this magnificent curry!
